name: Dream Portal
description: We built a 2 way connection bridge between virtual and real attendees of a live-streamed music festival. Festival goers could chat on a microphone to a rotating selection of a virtual users

name: Day 1 Club
description: Real-time rendering of diamonds, gold, silver and bronze for fans of the most influential hip-hop playlist in the world

name: Second Sky
description: Kinect powered visuals for music festival attendees. Displayed on a 16ft 3D led screen

name: Veiled Visions
description: Veiled Visions combines mixed reality and AI to create a whimsical seance experience. Madame Lysandra gives you real responses and conjures images from beyond the veil.

name: Sustainable Horizons
description: Explore a vision of the future created with AI. Concept, script, prompt engineering, sound, and visuals were created in a suite of generative AI tools.

name: Wrapped
description: Taking listeners on a journey through how they listened from top songs and artists. We brought to data to life through a fun & interactive visual journey

name: Finding Love
description: An audio-visual love story experienced through five distinct chapters

name: Soundwaves
description: Live during SXSW as part of an annual showcase of XR experiences, winning the audience award. Experience it and all 14 volumetric performances as they play back to back.

name: New Frontier
description: A web and VR virtual event that ran alongside the Sundance Film Festival. 40,000 attendees watched films, explored an XR art gallery, and socialized through video and voice chat

name: The Field
description: A desktop, mobile, and VR experience showcasing sustainability and wellness stories at The Future of Everything Festival

name: Kandinsky
description: Transforms art into music. Users sketch on a web canvas and hear their drawings come to life via playful, responsive shapes

name: Currents
description: A WebVR experiment built for Oculus Quest, exploring unseen forces beneath the ocean's surface

name: Magic Leap
description: A series of experiments created with the Magic Leap team to explore device features and capabilities

name: Breakfast verse
description: A surreal multi-player environment where users navigate their way through the Breakfastverse, aiming to reach the Chrome Throne for their chance to win AMBUSH x REESE'S PUFFS collectibles

name: World Brush
description: An AR experience allowing users to anonymously paint in real-world locations, visible to others only at the point of creation

name: Nurture
description: An intimate multiplayer experience explores five unique environments, inspired by singles from Porter Robinson’s album Nurture

name: Singles
description: An immersive VR music and art gallery featuring tracks from Adult Swim Singles 2016

name: Deliciously Dark Escape
description: Worm your way through 5 glowing pixel art webgl levels and get eaten.

name: Chile 20
description: Working with adidas Originals to create and showcase realistic 3D products for the new CHILE20 collection. We built 3D clothing for website and in-store displays

name: 20 Years of Xbox
description: An immersive, multi-player microverse featuring six unique environments to celebrate Xbox's 20th anniversary. Users explore the console's history and generate a personal museum using Microsoft account data

name: Singularity
description: Celebrating the website that makes websites with Adam Driver. The Singularity is upon us

name: Harmonic State
description: Explore three WebGL levels and learn how IBM transforms business operations from dissonance to harmony through Watson’s critical insights

name: Prometheus
description: A stylized interactive story and learn how Prometheus Fuels is creating fuel from the air.

name: Cardano Summit
description: A custom-built Dreamwave Microverse event for Cardano and IOHK, unveiling the latest in blockchain innovation. Over 140,000 attendees explored seven worlds with collectible NFTs, and engaged with 208 speakers across 70+ hours of content

name: Dreamwave
description: Born from the pandemic of 2020. Dreamwave is a technology and multiplayer platform for socialized experiences that span across Desktop, Mobile & XR. Dreamwave technology can facilitate online gatherings in the millions

name: Iconic Mints
description: A multi-platform experience that reimagines the art gallery, stroll a collection of artworks and explore the stories behind them through immersive visuals, interactive content, and audio guides. Our largest environment built to date

name: SILVER FCTRY
description: A fashion-centric metaverse that serves as a web-based HQ for AMBUSH's latest collections, creative vision and virtual events. Powered by Active Theory's Dreamwave platform

name: Secret Sky
description: A virtual music festival that ran for 10 hours and featured 16 artists. 160,000 attendees experienced the music in a multi-player 3D environment from a combination of Desktop, Mobile and VR

name: Watson Masters
description: IBM's Watson responds to sight, voice, and commands of tour guides at the Masters tournament, delivering real-time statistics onto a curved u-shaped room

name: Climatune
description: A series of playlists and visuals revealing how weather impacts music choices worldwide. In partnership with Spotify and Accuweather

name: Eye of the Stormers
description: A collaborative project between Bastille and Spotify, as a companion piece to their new album, Wild World

name: Bon Iver Viisualiizer
description: See how the world is listening to Bon Iver’s new album i,i with a living, breathing visualizer, made only of i’s.

name: Classic Stories Retold
description: We created a content hub to house over 60 videos from 13 advertising agencies that re-imagined classic fairy tales. Each story had an unique 3D model that would materialize through mesmerizing WebGL particle effects.

name: Mastered from Chaos
description: Experience the mastery of Hennessy through the interactive story of Hennessy V.S.O.P Privilege. Interactive WebGL lidar scans, comprised of hundreds of thousand of points, represent core aspects of the process.

name: Emmit Fenn
description: An interactive WebGL experience exploring nine interactive visuals - one for each track on Emmit's debut EP

name: Spacecraft for All
description: An interactive documentary blending real-time 3D graphics with video footage, telling the story of the recovered ISEE-3 satellite

name: I Will What I Want
description: A campaign site that displays dynamic social commentary composited in real-time alongside Gisele Bundchen

name: Acoustic Garage
description: A 3D car configurator for users to drive, customize and learn about automotive acoustic design

name: Witness Gotham
description: An explorable crime scene where users can collect evidence and attempt to solve the Wayne murders

name: Toonami
description: Explores the world of Toonami through an epic WebGL timeline of images, videos and sound bites. We built 3 consecutive annual versions each involving a new visual direction

name: Halo 5 Visualizer
description: Visualizes trailer debuts, social commentary, and user-generated content with particles forming Master Chief's helmet. Features include spatial audio particles, interactive lighting, and a draggable timeline

name: Rick and Morty
description: Dive into the world of Rick and Morty with an interactive experience that uses art, characters and assets from the show’s universe

name: Discover your Patronus
description: A visually immersive quiz for fans to discover their own personalized Patronus guardian
stats: The experience has been viewed over 3 million times across YouTube, with participants ranging YouTube influencers to Harry Potter cast members.

name: Airman Challenge
description: A high-tech, interactive training simulation with 16 unique mini-games, designed to teach potential recruits about different career paths within the Air Force
stats: Since launch, more than 365,000 users have played nearly 725,000 missions, spending an average of 6.5 minutes engaging with the brand.

name: Open Source Rover
description: Features two fully drivable rovers in an explorable Mars environment, reaching over 4 million people on NASA's social media at launch

name: Beat That
description: A racing game celebrating 20 years of driving history, featuring six races, six cars, and six environments, with graphics evolving from 8-bit styling to modern console standards. A dynamic leaderboard encourages competition

name: Welcome to Hogwarts
description: An immersive 3D recreation of Hogwarts where users can explore the world of Harry Potter

name: Wonderful Weekends
description: A playful 'gumball' experience and visual guide for discovering new activities using Google's product suite, mirroring the physical installation at a Chicago festival

name: Million Piece Mission
description: A collaborative virtual puzzle with 1.2 million pieces. Users work together to select and place 3,000 pieces, earning points, leaderboard rankings, and unlocking content

name: Adventure Time BMO
description: Users interact with BMO, a beloved Adventure Time character, to unlock mini-games and interactive moments such as potato pong and a shareable beat maker

name: Screamlands
description: A thrilling WebGL ride through a haunted house, promoting Huluween with spooky scenes inspired by Hulu's TV shows and movies

name: Look at the Sky
description: A five-minute, multiplayer music video accessible via mobile and webVR, immersing fans in Porter's latest track

name: E.C.H.O.
description: A cinematic WebGL experience offering guided levels to test and enhance cognitive skills

name: Tarot with YouTube
description: Celebrating World Tarot Day with YouTube, an experience that seamlessly combines videos and user interaction to create a personalized tarot reading and shareable Soul Card

thumbnail.filename: FWA_ Project Slide 1 - Same As Thumbnail-400x300.png

name: Doja Code
description: Introducing Doja Cat fans to basic programming languages through the ability to modify a music video with code

name: Our Planet
description: Users engage with an interactive flexible screen to learn about our impact on the natural environment. Featured at Netflix’s FYSEE event

name: Museum of Weed
description: An interactive exhibit that follows the road to legalization, featuring a 30 foot timeline controlled via Kinect

name: Frontier Within
description: An installation that captures the participant's circulatory, respiratory and nervous system data, and transforms it into a living, breathing, interactive portrait of the body

name: Bubbles
description: A global interactive feature at Google's 2017 Developer Conference, where users blow into their microphones to form bubbles that travel to the event's big screen

name: Mira
description: An exploration in cross-platform development where touch generates meditative sounds and visuals. Created for web, native mobile apps, Apple TV, VR, and physical installations

name: Neon
description: An experimental installation blending complex neon graphics with live user input via a Kinect. Created for an Active Theory holiday party

name: Touch Lobby
description: An interactive installation for Blue Shield’s San Francisco Lobby. WebGL, Electron and multi-touch hardware were used to tell the story across a massive 22-foot screen

name: Racer
description: A multi-device racing experience that syncs in real-time. Developed for Google’s I/O developer conference

name: World Draw
description: Sketch something, turn it into an object and place into an ever expanding world. Launched at Google I/O 2018, World Draw has attracted over 300,000 creators from all over the world.

name: Paper Planes
description: A multi-player experience that lets users create, throw and catch paper planes. Created to open the Google IO conference in 2016,  users have continued to engage - downloading the app over 500,000 times
stats: 14 million planes thrown and caught, 158 countries getting involved, 2:16 minutes average interaction time
